name: Clay County Bank, INC.
description_short: Community bank in Clay, West Virginia offering checking, savings, CDs, loans, and online banking with two local branches and 24/7 digital access.
description_long: Clay County Bank is a community-focused financial institution based in Clay, West Virginia, serving residents and businesses in the region. The bank operates two physical locations—a main office on Main Street in Clay and a branch office in Two Run—and has been rooted in the local Appalachian community. Founded on principles of personal service and relationship banking, Clay County Bank positions itself as a small-town alternative to larger regional banks. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including checking and savings accounts, CDs and IRAs, consumer and business loans, and online banking services accessible 24/7 through their myCCB platform and mobile app. Operating hours are Monday through Friday 8 AM to 5 PM at the main office (with extended Friday drive-thru hours until 6 PM) and Saturday 8 AM to 12 PM. They emphasize combining traditional community banking values with modern technology and security features. Clay County Bank distinguishes itself through its explicit commitment to local community engagement and personalized customer service. The website emphasizes the bank's knowledge of local affairs, connection to regional attractions (Golden Delicious Festival, Elk River Trail State Park), and availability at two convenient locations. They also highlight specific security measures to protect customers from fraud schemes, demonstrating awareness of current threats targeting rural banking customers. As a small community bank, Clay County Bank is best suited for residents and businesses in Clay County, West Virginia seeking personalized relationship banking with local decision-making. However, customers in other regions will not have branch access, and the bank's product offerings and rates are not publicly detailed on the website, requiring direct contact for specific terms. The institution appears stable and committed to its community role, though the limited geographic footprint means it is not practical for customers outside West Virginia.
pros: Two convenient local branch locations in Clay, West Virginia with extended drive-thru hours
24/7 online banking access through myCCB platform and mobile app download
Explicit security protocols in place to prevent social engineering fraud and account takeover
Small-town, personalized customer service approach with emphasis on relationship banking
Routing number provided (051502641) for easy account setup and transfers
Currently hiring and actively engaged in community development
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, and loan products
cons: Limited to Clay County, West Virginia geography—only two branch locations with no online-only access for out-of-state customers
No specific interest rates, APYs, loan terms, or fee structures published on website
Small bank size may mean limited product features or competitive rates compared to larger regional banks
Website lacks transparency on minimum account balances, overdraft policies, or loan application requirements
No mention of FDIC insurance, financial strength ratings, or regulatory compliance details on public-facing content
best_for: Clay County, West Virginia residents and businesses seeking personalized community banking
Customers prioritizing local decision-making and relationship-based service over large bank convenience
Businesses in the Appalachian region needing commercial banking services with local branch access
Individuals seeking to support local community institutions while maintaining modern online banking access
diagnosis: Clay County Bank is ideal for residents and small business owners in Clay, West Virginia who value personalized, local banking relationships and want 24/7 digital access alongside in-person service. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—the bank operates only two branches in Clay County, making it impractical for customers outside West Virginia, and critical product details like rates and fees require direct contact.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
